---
pagetitle: 'IIF OSF 2025: 25 years of open source forecasting software'
date: 2025-06-26 09:05:00
time: 09:05 AM
title: 25 years of open source forecasting software
description: Open source forecasting software has evolved from a few basic R functions
  to a rich ecosystem of R, Python, Julia, and C/C++ implementations of established
  models. In this session, the historical development of these tools will be reviewed
  and key gaps and areas requiring further attention will be highlighted.
speakers: Rob Hyndman
---

::: {#sponsors_banner .g-col-12 .title}
# 25 years of open source forecasting software

### June 26, 09:05 AM
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .full_text_block style="min-width: 640px; max-width:1080px; margin: 20px auto"}

Twenty-five years ago, the only open source forecasting software available were the HoltWinters(), arima() and StructTS() functions in R. Now we have many R packages available that are widely used, a burgeoning number of Python packages, and a few Julia packages, along with C or C++ implementations of ETS, ARIMA and other well-known models. I will discuss the development of open source forecasting software over the years, and highlight areas needing attention.

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IawB-TalQv8 >}}

:::


```{=html}
<style>
#title-block-header {
display: none !important;
}
</style>
```
